channel.title: Programming on michael-0acf4
channel.description: Recent content in Programming on michael-0acf4

item.title: Option is a monad
item.description:
Let’s dive a bit into a little Haskell journey to prove that the well known Option data type, can behave like a Monad, and along the way, we’ll see how it can also be a Functor, Applicative, and more.

What is a monad?

For practical purposes, let’s just see what it does. Then try to define it later.

Step 1: Defining the Option Type

To begin, we define our type:
